proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Semantic Correctness (calling console.log() without arguments is always safe)
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Preconditions: runExplain has no preconditions that affect this call.
; Transition: the statement `console.log()` executes.  This has no
;             effect on the program state that can cause a fault,
;             so we model it as a no‑op (`true`).
; Property to prove: the execution cannot raise an exception or
;                    violate any safety condition.
; Negation of the property (i.e., “an exception is raised”) is modeled
; as `false`.
(assert true)        ; model the successful execution of console.log()
(assert false)       ; negated safety property – “an error occurs”
(check-sat)          ; expecting UNSAT → the error condition is unreachable

; PRINCIPLE: P3 – Precondition Propagation (no precondition is required for console.log)
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; The only “precondition” that could be relevant is that the JavaScript
; runtime provides a `console` object with a `log` method.  This is
; guaranteed by the host environment, so we treat it as true.
; To show that a missing precondition is *not* reachable we assert the
; precondition and then assert its violation; the conjunction is
; unsatisfiable.
(assert true)                ; console.log is available
(assert (not true))          ; violation: console.log is unavailable
(check-sat)                  ; expects UNSAT
